item.title: The trend of obesity and inflammatory bowel disease in Australia and New Zealand: a bi-nation cohort study.
item.description: Internal medicine journal - The paradigm of inflammatory bowel disease is shifting from a malabsorptive, undernourished state to one where obesity is a prevalent comorbidity, yet large-scale Australasian data are lacking. We ...

item.title: Outcomes of Root Canal Treatment in Patients With Autoimmune Disease: A Retrospective Case-Control Study.
item.description: International endodontic journal - The purpose of this study was to compare the outcome of nonsurgical root canal treatment (RCT) in patients with autoimmune diseases (AD) with the outcome in patients without AD. The null hypothesis...

item.title: Safety of IL-17A Inhibitors in Patients With Moderate to Severe Psoriasis in a US Claims Data-Based Cohort Study.
item.description: Journal of psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis - Safety outcomes from IL-17A inhibitors have not been fully evaluated. Compare the risk of seven safety outcomes in patients with psoriasis who started treatment with either ixekizumab or secukinuma...

item.title: Sexual dysfunction is highly prevalent in patients with active perianal fistulizing Crohn's disease: Outcomes from a large national prospective cohort study.
item.description: Colorectal disease : the official journal of the Association of Coloproctology of Great Britain and Ireland - Multicenter study - Sexual function is one of the determinants of quality of life (QoL) and is prevalent in approximately half of the patients with inflammatory bowel disease. However, the sexual function in patients ...

item.title: Extraintestinal manifestations in Korean patients with inflammatory bowel disease: A nationwide population-based study from 2005 to 2017.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Extraintestinal manifestations (EIMs) significantly contribute to morbidity in individuals with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD); however, population-based East Asian data remain limited. We evalua...

item.title: Advanced adenomas among young endurance runners: A prospective hypothesis-generating prevalence study.
item.description: Cancer epidemiology - Extreme long-distance running can produce recurrent gastrointestinal stress, including splanchnic hypoperfusion, mucosal injury, and post-exercise rectal bleeding, but its relationship to colorecta...

item.title: The incidence and prevalence of paediatric inflammatory bowel disease in Scotland: A nationwide study from 2015 to 2018.
item.description: Journal of pediatric gastroenterology and nutrition - Scotland is known to have the highest incidence of paediatric-onset inflammatory bowel disease (PIBD) in the United Kingdom and one of the highest worldwide; however, data on prevalence is lacking....

item.title: Association of Crohn's disease with periodontal disease risk and severity: a meta-analytic study.
item.description: Acta odontologica Scandinavica - Meta-analysis - Crohn's disease (CD) is a chronic inflammatory disorder with systemic manifestations, including potential effects on oral health. Evidence regarding its association with periodontal disease remains...

item.title: Frailty and geriatric complications in older patients with IBD: a nationwide hospital claims database study across Japan.
item.description: Aging clinical and experimental research - Older patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) often have multimorbidity and functional vulnerability, which increases the risk of in-hospital complications. This study examined whether frail...

item.title: Associations of prenatal exposure to maternal autoimmune disorders with a wide spectrum of psychiatric and neurodevelopmental disorders in offspring-a nationwide cohort study.
item.description: Human reproduction open - Are children exposed in utero to various maternal autoimmune disorders (ADs) and autoinflammatory disorders (AIDs) at greater risk for developing any of a wide range of psychiatric or neurodevelopm...

item.title: Analgesic Use in Patients with Mild Inflammatory Bowel Disease-A Nationwide Cohort Study Based on Prescription Data.
item.description: Journal of clinical medicine - Background: In patients with Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC) disease severity and activity have been associated with pain. Data on analgesic use in patients with mild disease, howe...

item.title: Development and validation of a predictive nomogram for response to biologics and targeted therapy in Crohn's disease: a retrospective cohort study.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Biologics and targeted therapies are a first-line treatment option for individuals with Crohn's disease (CD). Nevertheless, clinical remission rates vary considerably among different biologics and ...
